stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and llama ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the transformers and safetensors ecosystems.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
The attack exploits positional encoding within LLMs as a trigger, moving beyond traditional content-based triggers.
Highlights a critical security vulnerability in LLMs, especially those used in safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model performance.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the core ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, offering faster local AI inference on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open usage.

stories.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to all adult users (18+).
The feature uses a selfie-style scan to monitor the platform for potential deepfakes of the user.
If a match is found, YouTube will alert the user.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively using AI to combat insider trading.
The focus of this AI application is specifically within prediction markets.
This signals a significant regulatory shift toward leveraging advanced AI for financial market integrity.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5B.
The settlement process is facing delays due to judicial scrutiny.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the deal to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a new framework for LLMs.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static after pretraining by allowing dynamic knowledge incorporation.
Achieves this by encoding new, domain-specific knowledge into a dedicated memory model without altering the core LLM parameters.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Identifies a 'systematic dual failure' where gradient-based unlearning methods lose their effect under 4-bit compression, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.
Proposes 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning' as a necessary advancement to ensure model forgetting persists in deployed, compressed models.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective threat coverage of existing LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in the STRIDE threat model, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The methodology synthesizes findings from 932 arXiv security studies (2023-2026) to validate benchmark completeness.

stories.title: SANA-WM, a 2.6B open-source world model for 1-minute 720p video
stories.author: mjgil
bullet_points: Introduction of SANA-WM, a new open-source world model.
The model is capable of generating 1-minute, 720p video content.
Its scale is significant, boasting 2.6 billion parameters.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models like Claude alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-source models, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have settled a lawsuit concerning the impact of social media on students.
The suit alleged that social media addiction has caused significant financial strain and disrupted learning in public schools.
The case was initiated by the Breathitt County School District in Kentucky, highlighting mental health concerns related to social media use.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The article discusses the increased surveillance and interest in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO) by the US, China, and Russia.
It highlights a technical vulnerability: most satellites are not as stealthy as assumed against the backdrop of space.
The narrative suggests a shift from covert operations to more visible, competitive activity in space.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge in video generation: maintaining entity (character, object, location) consistency across long sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more rigorous evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses two approaches to visual reasoning: agentic reasoning (using external tools/code) and latent reasoning (using hidden embeddings).
Direct image generation during reasoning is noted as computationally expensive and architecturally complex.
A key trade-off is highlighted between agentic methods' context-switching latency and the complexity of latent methods.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying actual, chronological world events (like news articles) to ground agent interactions.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their knowledge cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a feed-forward native method for 3D scene editing, moving beyond static scene generation.
It addresses the limitation of current models which struggle to respond to dynamic human instructions.
The method aims to overcome existing 2D-lifting strategies by enabling direct 3D manipulation.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of videos generated by AI world models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide diagnostic metrics for geometric failures in generated video.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in training large, unified Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The problem arises from co-training disparate physical simulation regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media) which cause gradient conflicts.
The proposed solution involves using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible demands on a single model path.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a new framework for improving LLM reasoning by scaling compute breadth.
Addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by using pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation instead of noisy pointwise judging.
Focuses on test-time compute scaling, a critical area for advancing LLM capabilities beyond simple depth extension.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways for better clinical decision support.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured data for better patient trajectory modeling.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are deemed insufficient to verify complex safety claims required by modern AI governance.
Current frameworks demand evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Existing assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemic gap.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework for testing AI agents in dynamic environments.
The core concept involves replaying real-world events chronologically to evaluate an agent's adaptability.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper investigates how different retrieval strategies interact with LLM agent architectures in complex, autonomous workflows.
It addresses a gap in current literature regarding the systematic comparison of retrieval choice against agent design and tool-calling methods.
The research focuses on improving agentic search systems, which are increasingly relying on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes using a Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment approach to combine unstructured narrative data with structured EHR data.
The goal is to improve patient trajectory modeling and risk forecasting for complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework specifically designed for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current research where agents often rely only on textual traces instead of preserving fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, which is currently underrepresented in evaluations.

stories.title: I Added Three Rules to Gemma 4. The MoE Searched. The Dense Model Refused.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: The article compares Gemma 4's Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) and dense architectures against GPT-4o variants in a real-world Arabic e-commerce chatbot scenario.
A specific set of three custom prompt rules caused the MoE variant to successfully open a catalog while the dense variant failed with the same prompt.
The findings highlight potential architectural differences (MoE vs. Dense) in handling complex, language-specific tasks.
